mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly lifting equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer demand, which compresses corporate revenue expectations and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the rising unemployment signal, or the predictive Rank IC exceeds 0.05 in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent declines in housing starts and retail sales indicate a synchronized contraction in consumer spending and business investment, which reduces forward earnings guidance and pushes S&P 500 returns negative over a monthly horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative returns in more than 50% of observations, or the interaction term in a multivariate regression shows a p-value greater than 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained expansion in industrial production reflects robust manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which supports corporate profit margins and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns do not exhibit a statistically significant positive mean difference when INDPRO growth exceeds its 12-month moving average, or the signal's Sharpe ratio falls below 0.20.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating real GDP growth expands the aggregate earnings base for public companies, directly translating into higher equity valuations and positive S&P 500 forward returns over a one-week horizon.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDP revisions consistently move in the opposite direction of subsequent 5-day SPX returns, or the rolling Rank IC remains negative across all available data periods.

mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rates exceeding 4.5% impose a restrictive monetary stance that elevates borrowing costs and suppresses equity risk premiums, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a monthly horizon.
failure_condition: Equity returns remain positive or neutral in more than 55% of instances where the funds rate stays above 4.5% for consecutive months, or the signal's directional accuracy drops below 50%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous payroll growth deceleration and unemployment rate elevation signal a labor market inflection point that precedes broader economic slowdown, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 3-month MA declines while UNRATE rises
failure_condition: The joint labor deterioration signal fails to precede SPX drawdowns exceeding 5% in more than 40% of historical instances, or the interaction coefficient in a factor model is statistically indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts indicate renewed consumer confidence and forward-looking investment activity, which boosts construction-related earnings and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a one-week horizon.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns are not significantly positive when housing starts accelerate, or the signal produces a negative Rank IC over any 12-month rolling window.
